relevance_summary: CXCR2 is implicated in the inflammatory response associated with Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), making it a promising candidate biomarker for early diagnosis.
citations: Bioinformatics analysis and identification of genes and molecular pathways in steroid-induced osteonecrosis of the femoral head. (2021). https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34016144/

relevance_summary: P2RY13 is a potential biomarker for early detection of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) due to its association with immune mechanisms involved in the disease.
citations: Identification of key biomarkers in steroid-induced osteonecrosis of the femoral head and their correlation with immune infiltration by bioinformatics analysis. (2022). https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35042504/

relevance_summary: NLRP1 is a key regulator of pyroptosis and inflammation, making it a significant biomarker for early detection of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH).

relevance_summary: TYROBP is a key immune-related biomarker associated with the pathogenesis of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH).
